gene editing.news: ProQR Therapeutics announced positive Phase 1 target engagement data for its candidate AX-0810. This milestone establishes the first clinical validation of the company's Axiomer RNA editing platform, which utilizes ADAR machinery to make specific single nucleotide edits in RNA.

bispecific antibodies.news: WuXi Biologics announced that its 25th drug substance facility (MFG17) in Shanghai successfully completed its first GMP production campaign with zero deviations. The facility is equipped to support the manufacturing of complex biologics, including bispecific antibodies, utilizing flexible single-use technologies and advanced continuous manufacturing capabilities.

WuXi Biologics' Suzhou Biosafety Testing Center received its fourth EMA GMP certification following a successful inspection with zero critical findings. This certification enables the release of 19 commercial biologics for the European market, a portfolio that includes complex modalities such as bispecific antibodies developed on the company's integrated platforms.

monoclonal antibodies.news: WuXi Biologics announced that its 25th drug substance facility, MFG17 in Shanghai, successfully completed its first GMP production campaign with zero deviations. The facility is equipped to support the manufacturing of monoclonal antibodies and other biologics with a capacity of 9,000 liters for 80-100 batches annually.

The Suzhou Biosafety Testing Center of WuXi Biologics received its fourth EMA GMP certification following a successful inspection with zero critical findings. This certification enables the release of 19 commercial biologics products, the majority of which are monoclonal antibodies, for the European market.

The FDA approved Pfizer's IBRANCE (palbociclib) regimen in combination with the monoclonal antibodies trastuzumab and pertuzumab for the frontline maintenance treatment of HR+, HER2+ metastatic breast cancer. This approval, based on Phase 3 PATINA trial data, marks the first CDK4/6 inhibitor indication for patients regardless of HER2 status.

The European Commission granted marketing authorization for Trodelvy (sacituzumab govitecan-hziy) as a first-line treatment for metastatic triple-negative breast cancer in patients not candidates for PD-(L)1 inhibitors, based on Phase 3 ASCENT-03 data.

The U.S. FDA approved Trodelvy (sacituzumab govitecan-hziy) for the first-line treatment of metastatic triple-negative breast cancer, either as a monotherapy or in combination with Keytruda (pembrolizumab), based on ASCENT-03 and ASCENT-04 trial results.

The European Commission approved PADCEV (enfortumab vedotin) in combination with Keytruda (pembrolizumab) as the first perioperative treatment option for cisplatin-ineligible patients with resectable muscle-invasive bladder cancer, based on Phase 3 EV-303 results.

gene editing.news: Genetix Biotherapeutics and SPIMACO announced an exclusive strategic agreement to commercialize and manufacture the gene addition therapies LYFGENIA and ZYNTEGLO in Saudi Arabia and the broader Middle East. The partnership includes localized manufacturing capabilities to address sickle cell disease and beta-thalassemia in the region.

bispecific antibodies.news: Sartorius and LFB BIOMANUFACTURING announced an expansion of their collaboration to offer an integrated solution from cell line development to GMP drug substance manufacturing. The partnership specifically targets the acceleration of development for complex biologics, including bispecific antibodies, by combining Sartorius' cell line expertise with LFB's manufacturing capabilities.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Pfizer announced topline Phase 3 results for sigvotatug vedotin, an integrin beta-6 directed antibody-drug conjugate (ADC), in previously treated metastatic non-squamous non-small cell lung cancer. While the study did not meet the primary endpoint of overall survival in the overall population, a stronger trend was observed in patients with only one prior line of therapy, supporting continued evaluation in combination with immunotherapy.

The European Commission approved SKYRIZI (risankizumab), an IL-23 inhibitor monoclonal antibody, for treating moderate to severe plaque psoriasis in pediatric patients aged six and older. The approval is based on Phase 3 OptIMMize-1 and OptIMMize-2 trials and includes a new 55 mg pre-filled syringe for weight-based dosing.

AbbVie announced a definitive agreement to acquire Apogee Therapeutics for approximately $10.9 billion, adding a pipeline of clinical-stage monoclonal antibodies including zumilokibart (APG777) targeting IL-13 for atopic dermatitis and a combination therapy targeting IL-13 and TSLP for asthma.

AbbVie announced a definitive agreement to acquire Apogee Therapeutics for approximately $10.9 billion, gaining access to a pipeline of half-life extended monoclonal antibodies. The deal includes zumilokibart (APG777), a late-stage anti-IL-13 antibody for atopic dermatitis, and APG273, a combination antibody targeting IL-13 and TSLP for asthma.

Sanofi's Sarclisa (isatuximab) subcutaneous formulation received approval in Japan for treating multiple myeloma, based on the pivotal IRAKLIA phase 3 study. The approval covers combinations with pomalidomide/dexamethasone for relapsed/refractory cases and bortezomib/lenalidomide/dexamethasone for newly diagnosed patients.

Sanofi's Sarclisa (isatuximab) subcutaneous formulation received approval in Japan for treating multiple myeloma. The approval covers use in combination with standard-of-care regimens for both newly diagnosed and relapsed or refractory patients, based on data from the pivotal IRAKLIA phase 3 study.

WuXi Biologics' Suzhou Biosafety Testing Center received its fourth EMA GMP certification, enabling the commercialization of 19 biologics for the European market. The approved products include monoclonal antibodies developed and manufactured on WuXi Biologics' integrated platform.

The FDA accepted a supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for Genentech's combination of Lunsumio (mosunetuzumab) and Polivy (polatuzumab vedotin) for treating relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma. The filing is based on Phase III SUNMO study data showing a 59% reduction in the risk of disease progression or death compared to standard chemotherapy.

Chugai received regulatory approval in Japan for an additional indication of Avastin (bevacizumab), a humanized anti-VEGF monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of neurofibromatosis type 2 (NF2). This approval, based on the BeatNF2 Phase II clinical study, marks the first therapeutic drug approval globally for this rare disease.

Zenyaku Kogyo obtained regulatory approval in Japan for an additional indication of Rituxan (rituximab), an anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of adult-onset frequently relapsing or steroid-dependent nephrotic syndrome. The approval follows positive results from an investigator-initiated Phase III clinical trial showing a significantly higher relapse-free rate compared to placebo.

Asimov announced a strategic partnership with the Rosalind & Morris Goodman Cancer Institute at McGill University to provide cell line and process development services for next-generation biologic candidates, including complex multispecifics and standard IgGs. The collaboration aims to support researchers in advancing promising biotherapeutic molecules through IND-enabling studies using Asimov's AI-powered CHO Edge System.

Asimov announced a cell line development partnership with Score Pharma to advance biosuperior antibody therapeutics. The collaboration leverages Asimov's platform to support the development and optimization of antibody candidates.

bispecific antibodies.news: The FDA has accepted a supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for Genentech's subcutaneous bispecific antibody Lunsumio VELO (mosunetuzumab-axgb) in combination with Polivy for treating relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma. The filing is based on Phase III SUNMO study data showing a 59% reduction in the risk of disease progression or death compared to standard chemotherapy.

The FDA has accepted a supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for Roche's subcutaneous bispecific antibody Lunsumio (mosunetuzumab) in combination with Polivy for treating relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma. The filing is based on positive Phase III SUNMO study data showing a 59% reduction in the risk of disease progression or death compared to standard chemotherapy.

UCB completed the acquisition of Candid Therapeutics for up to $2.2 billion, bringing cizutamig into its pipeline. Cizutamig is a bispecific antibody targeting BCMA and CD3 designed to deplete pathogenic B-cell populations in autoimmune diseases.

Asimov announced a strategic partnership with the Rosalind & Morris Goodman Cancer Institute at McGill University to advance next-generation biologics, specifically supporting the development of complex multispecific architectures including bispecific antibodies through its AI-powered CHO Edge System.

The FDA accepted a supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for Roche's subcutaneous formulation of the bispecific antibody Lunsumio (mosunetuzumab) in combination with Polivy for treating relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma. The filing is based on phase III SUNMO study data showing a 59% reduction in the risk of disease progression or death compared to standard chemotherapy.

UCB completed the acquisition of Candid Therapeutics for up to $2.2 billion, adding cizutamig to its pipeline. Cizutamig is a BCMA x CD3 bispecific antibody currently in Phase 1 clinical studies for autoimmune diseases.

UCB completed the acquisition of Candid Therapeutics for up to $2.2 billion, bringing cizutamig into its pipeline. Cizutamig is a BCMA x CD3 bispecific antibody currently in Phase 1 clinical studies for autoimmune diseases.

UCB completed the acquisition of Candid Therapeutics for up to $2.2 billion, bringing cizutamig into its pipeline. Cizutamig is a bispecific antibody targeting BCMA and CD3 designed to deplete pathogenic B-cell populations for autoimmune diseases.

monoclonal antibodies.news: The FDA has granted Priority Review to AstraZeneca's supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for Ultomiris (ravulizumab) for the treatment of adults with immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN). The decision is based on positive interim results from the Phase III I CAN trial, which showed a significant reduction in proteinuria compared to placebo.

The US FDA has granted Priority Review to AstraZeneca's Alexion for Ultomiris (ravulizumab), a C5 complement inhibitor monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of adults with immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN). The decision is based on Phase III I CAN trial results showing a 43.4% reduction in proteinuria versus placebo, with a regulatory decision expected in Q4 2026.

The US FDA granted accelerated approval to Tzield (teplizumab-mzwv), a CD3-directed monoclonal antibody, to delay the decline in endogenous insulin production in children aged 8 to 17 recently diagnosed with stage 3 type 1 diabetes. The approval is based on data from the PROTECT phase 3 study and expands the drug's indication to include younger pediatric patients.

Chugai Pharmaceutical filed a regulatory application in Japan for Tecentriq (atezolizumab), a humanized anti-PD-L1 monoclonal antibody, for an additional indication as maintenance therapy following definitive chemoradiotherapy in locally advanced esophageal cancer. The filing is based on positive results from the global Phase III SKYSCRAPER-07 clinical trial.

Chugai filed a regulatory application in Japan for Tecentriq (atezolizumab), a humanized anti-PD-L1 monoclonal antibody, for an additional indication as maintenance therapy following definitive chemoradiotherapy in locally advanced esophageal cancer. The filing is based on positive results from the global Phase III SKYSCRAPER-07 clinical trial.

Novartis announced that the FORTITUDE Phase I/II study of del-brax, an investigational antibody oligonucleotide conjugate (AOC) combining monoclonal antibody tissue specificity with oligonucleotide precision, met its primary biomarker endpoint in treating facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy (FSHD). The results demonstrated strong target engagement and reduction in muscle damage, validating the dosing regimen for the ongoing Phase III study.

Novartis announced that the FORTITUDE Phase I/II study of del-brax, an investigational antibody oligonucleotide conjugate (AOC) combining monoclonal antibodies with siRNA, met its primary biomarker endpoint in treating facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy (FSHD). The results demonstrated significant reduction in DUX4-regulated biomarkers and muscle damage markers, validating the dosing regimen for the ongoing Phase III trial.

The FDA granted Priority Review to Genentech's supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for Tecentriq (atezolizumab) in combination with chemotherapy for stage III dMMR/MSI-H colon cancer. The decision is based on Phase III ATOMIC study results showing a 50% reduction in recurrence or death risk, with an approval decision expected by October 9, 2026.

BigHat Biosciences appointed Stefan Weigand as Chief Scientific Officer to lead its AI-driven platform for designing complex protein therapeutics, including antibodies. Weigand brings over 25 years of experience in biologics research from Roche, where he oversaw the development of numerous antibody-based programs.

bispecific antibodies.news: BigHat Biosciences appointed Stefan Weigand as Chief Scientific Officer. Weigand previously led the team at Roche that established the CrossMab platform and T-cell bispecifics, enabling over 60 molecules to advance into clinical development.

gene editing.news: Novartis announced that the FORTITUDE Phase I/II study of del-brax, an investigational antibody oligonucleotide conjugate (AOC) targeting the DUX4 gene, met its primary biomarker endpoint in patients with facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy (FSHD). The therapy, acquired via the Avidity Biosciences deal, demonstrated significant reduction in DUX4-regulated biomarkers and muscle damage markers, validating the dosing regimen for the ongoing Phase III trial.

monoclonal antibodies.news: The U.S. FDA has accepted Takeda's supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for intravenous ENTYVIO (vedolizumab) for the treatment of moderately to severely active ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease in pediatric patients aged 2 years and older. The application is supported by data from the KEPLER and WEBB Phase 3 trials, with a PDUFA goal date set for the first quarter of 2027.

Sanofi announced the termination of the MOBILIZE phase 3 study for riliprubart, an IgG4 humanized monoclonal antibody targeting C1s, in patients with chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy (CIDP) due to insufficient efficacy in an interim analysis.

The U.S. FDA has accepted Takeda's supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for intravenous ENTYVIO (vedolizumab) for the treatment of moderately to severely active ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease in pediatric patients aged 2 years and older. The application is supported by data from the KEPLER and WEBB Phase 3 clinical trials, with a PDUFA goal date set for the first quarter of 2027.

bispecific antibodies.news: The U.S. FDA approved an expanded indication for HYMPAVZI (marstacimab-hncq), a bispecific antibody targeting TFPI, for the treatment of hemophilia A or B in patients aged 12 and older with inhibitors, as well as pediatric patients aged 6 to 11 with or without inhibitors.

AbbVie presented new data at EHA 2026 for epcoritamab (EPKINLY), a CD3×CD20 bispecific antibody, including Phase 3 results from the EPCORE DLBCL-1 trial showing improved progression-free survival in relapsed/refractory large B-cell lymphoma compared to chemoimmunotherapy.

AbbVie presented new clinical data at EHA 2026 for epcoritamab (EPKINLY), a CD3×CD20 bispecific antibody, including Phase 3 results from the EPCORE DLBCL-1 trial showing improved progression-free survival in relapsed/refractory large B-cell lymphoma compared to chemoimmunotherapy. Additional data highlighted efficacy in follicular lymphoma and long-term remission rates.

AbbVie presented new clinical data at EHA 2026 for epcoritamab (EPKINLY), a CD3×CD20 bispecific antibody. Results from the Phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1 trial showed statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival compared to chemoimmunotherapy in patients with relapsed/refractory large B-cell lymphoma.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Amgen presented new Phase 3 VESALIUS-CV subgroup results at the American Diabetes Association 86th Scientific Sessions, showing that Repatha (evolocumab), a human monoclonal antibody, reduced the risk of first major cardiovascular events by 29% in patients with high-risk diabetes. The data, based on 6,002 patients, demonstrated significant reductions in composite endpoints including coronary heart disease death, myocardial infarction, and ischemic stroke when Repatha was added to standard lipid-lowering therapies.

GSK has entered an agreement to acquire Nuvalent, Inc. for $10.6 billion, a deal that includes Ris-Rez, a B7-H3 antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) currently in phase III clinical development. The acquisition aims to accelerate GSK's entry into lung cancer and expand its portfolio of targeted oncology therapies.

The U.S. FDA approved an expanded indication for HYMPAVZI (marstacimab-hncq), a bispecific monoclonal antibody, to include patients with hemophilia A or B ages 12 and older with inhibitors, as well as pediatric patients ages 6 to 11 with or without inhibitors. The approval covers routine prophylaxis for bleeding episodes in these populations, supported by Phase 3 BASIS and BASIS KIDS trial data.

Merck and Gilead announced the discontinuation of the Phase 3 KEYNOTE-D46/EVOKE-03 study evaluating the combination of Gilead's Trodelvy and Merck's anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody KEYTRUDA (pembrolizumab) in metastatic non-small cell lung cancer. The decision followed a Data Monitoring Committee recommendation due to a lack of statistical significance in progression-free survival and an unlikely probability of achieving a significant overall survival benefit.

The European Commission approved Sarclisa (isatuximab) subcutaneous, an anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody, for multiple myeloma treatment. This approval marks the first time an anticancer therapy is administered via an on-body injector in the EU, offering flexible home or outpatient administration.

AstraZeneca announced positive interim results from the Phase III I CAN trial for Ultomiris (ravulizumab), a monoclonal antibody targeting the C5 complement protein. The study demonstrated a 43.4% reduction in proteinuria versus placebo in adults with immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN) at week 34, supporting its potential as a disease-modifying treatment.

AstraZeneca reported positive interim results from the Phase III I CAN trial for Ultomiris (ravulizumab), a monoclonal antibody targeting the C5 complement protein. The study demonstrated a 43.4% reduction in proteinuria versus placebo in adults with immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN) at 34 weeks, supporting its potential as a disease-modifying treatment.

Lupin received U.S. FDA approval for Ranluspec (ranibizumab), an interchangeable biosimilar to Lucentis. Ranibizumab is a recombinant humanized IgG1 monoclonal antibody fragment indicated for treating neovascular age-related macular degeneration and other retinal conditions.

The U.S. FDA has approved Lupin's Ranluspec (ranibizumab), an interchangeable biosimilar to Lucentis, for treating neovascular age-related macular degeneration and other retinal conditions. This approval covers both vial and pre-filled syringe presentations, marking Lupin's second U.S. biosimilar.

UCB presented new EULAR 2026 data demonstrating that its Fc-free monoclonal antibody, certolizumab pegol (CIMZIA), delivers consistent efficacy in rheumatoid arthritis regardless of rheumatoid factor levels. The findings provide mechanistic support for the drug's differentiated structure, which avoids immune complex formation and clearance issues seen with Fc-containing biologics.

bispecific antibodies.news: Gilead Sciences and Lakefront Biotherapeutics completed the acquisition of Ouro Medicines to advance gamgertamig, a clinical-stage BCMAxCD3 T cell engager bispecific antibody for autoimmune diseases. The drug has received Fast Track and Orphan Drug Designations from the FDA and is expected to enter registrational studies in 2027.

monoclonal antibodies.news: UCB and Biogen presented new data at EULAR 2026 for dapirolizumab pegol, an investigational humanized Fc-free Fab' fragment, showing potential to reduce flare rates and maintain disease control in systemic lupus erythematosus while allowing steroid tapering.

Amgen presented new Phase 3 MITIGATE trial data at EULAR 2026 demonstrating sustained efficacy and safety of UPLIZNA (inebilizumab), a humanized monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of IgG4-related disease. The data showed that patients continuing treatment achieved high rates of flare-free, glucocorticoid-free complete remission with no new safety signals.

Novartis announced positive Phase III REPLENISH trial results for Cosentyx (secukinumab), a fully human monoclonal antibody, in polymyalgia rheumatica (PMR). The study showed statistically significant sustained remission rates compared to placebo and reduced steroid use, with data published in the New England Journal of Medicine and submitted for regulatory review in the US, EU, and Japan.

UCB and Biogen presented new data at EULAR 2026 for dapirolizumab pegol, an investigational humanized Fc-free polyethylene glycol (PEG)-conjugated antigen-binding (Fab') fragment, showing potential to reduce flare rates and maintain disease control in systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) while allowing for steroid tapering.

Nuclera launched a new antibody triage service designed to overcome validation bottlenecks in AI-driven antibody discovery. The service utilizes cell-free protein synthesis to rapidly filter non-binders from AI-generated libraries before expensive mammalian expression, reducing timelines from weeks to days.

Sanofi announced that Biogen has joined accelRare, a free pre-diagnostic tool for rare diseases co-developed with Medvir. While the partnership focuses on diagnostic acceleration for conditions like Friedreich's ataxia, Sanofi's involvement leverages its broader biopharmaceutical ecosystem, which includes a significant portfolio of monoclonal antibody therapies for rare diseases.

UCB presented new long-term data at EULAR 2026 for BIMZELX (bimekizumab), a dual IL-17A and IL-17F inhibitor monoclonal antibody. The data from Phase 2/3 trials in psoriatic arthritis and axial spondyloarthritis demonstrated that early and sustained inflammation control leads to improved patient-reported outcomes and limits radiographic disease progression over three years.

Junshi Biosciences announced that the primary endpoints of event-free survival and major pathological response were met in the final analysis of the Phase 3 NEOTORCH study for toripalimab plus chemotherapy in resectable stage II-III NSCLC. The company plans to submit a supplemental new drug application to expand the approval to include stage II patients.

The National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) approved toripalimab in combination with disitamab vedotin for the first-line treatment of HER2-expressing locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma. This approval marks the 13th indication for toripalimab in China, based on positive Phase 3 RC48-C016 trial results.

bispecific antibodies.news: The European Commission has granted marketing authorization for Amgen's IMDYLLTRA (tarlatamab), a bispecific T-cell engager targeting DLL3 and CD3, for the treatment of extensive-stage small cell lung cancer. The approval is based on Phase 3 DeLLphi-304 trial results showing a 40% reduction in the risk of death compared to chemotherapy.

AstraZeneca is advancing rilvegostomig (AZD2936), a PD-1/TIGIT bispecific antibody, in combination with chemotherapy as an adjuvant therapy for biliary tract cancer and with bevacizumab for advanced hepatocellular carcinoma. The company is also developing AZD5863, a Claudin 18.2/CD3 T-cell engager bispecific antibody, which is currently in Phase I development for gastric and gastroesophageal junction cancers.

The European Commission granted marketing authorization for IMDYLLTRA (tarlatamab), a DLL3xCD3 bispecific T-cell engager, for the treatment of extensive-stage small cell lung cancer. The approval is based on Phase 3 DeLLphi-304 trial results showing a 40% reduction in the risk of death compared to chemotherapy.

monoclonal antibodies.news: The FDA has accepted Genentech's New Drug Application (NDA) for giredestrant, an oral selective estrogen receptor degrader (SERD), for the adjuvant treatment of ER-positive early-stage breast cancer. The filing, based on positive Phase III lidERA study results showing a 30% reduction in recurrence or death risk, is under priority review with a decision expected by November 30, 2026.

AstraZeneca announced positive Phase III results from the EMERALD-3 trial, showing that the combination of Imfinzi (durvalumab) and Imjudo (tremelimumab) with lenvatinib and TACE reduced the risk of disease progression or death by 30% in patients with unresectable liver cancer. Imfinzi and Imjudo are human monoclonal antibodies targeting PD-L1 and CTLA-4, respectively.

gene editing.news: OXB announced Cure Lowe Foundation as a selected recipient for its VectorXcellerator Programme, providing fully funded feasibility studies for early-stage gene therapy initiatives involving lentiviral vectors and AAV.

bispecific antibodies.news: Immunocore presented updated Phase 1 data for brenetafusp, an ImmTAC bispecific antibody targeting PRAME, at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting. The study showed an 87% six-month overall survival rate in heavily pretreated melanoma patients, supporting the selection of a 160 mcg dose for the ongoing Phase 3 PRISM-MEL-301 trial.

Immunocore presented updated Phase 1 data for brenetafusp, an ImmTAC bispecific antibody targeting PRAME, at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting. The data showed an 87% 6-month overall survival rate in heavily pretreated melanoma patients, supporting the selection of a 160 mcg dose for the ongoing Phase 3 PRISM-MEL-301 trial.

Immunocore presented updated Phase 1 data for brenetafusp, a PRAME-targeted ImmTAC bispecific antibody, at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting. The data showed an 87% 6-month overall survival rate in heavily pretreated melanoma patients, supporting the selection of a 160 mcg dose for the ongoing Phase 3 PRISM-MEL-301 trial.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Novartis will present new data for ianalumab, a monoclonal antibody, at EULAR 2026, including interim results from the Phase III extension study in Sjögren's disease up to week 108 and Phase II data in systemic lupus erythematosus.

New data from the REPLENISH Phase III trial for secukinumab (Cosentyx), a monoclonal antibody, will be highlighted at EULAR 2026, presenting results in polymyalgia rheumatica and subgroup analyses of Week 52 results.

UCB and Biogen announced the publication of positive Phase 3 results for dapirolizumab pegol, an investigational Fc-free CD40L inhibitor, in The Lancet. The study demonstrated statistically significant improvement in disease activity for systemic lupus erythematosus patients, supporting continued development and the ongoing confirmatory PHOENYCS FLY trial.

gene editing.news: Tune Therapeutics presented positive Phase 1b/2a data for TUNE-401, a first-in-class epigenetic silencer for Hepatitis B, at EASL 2026. The study demonstrated the first clinical evidence of direct epigenetic silencing of the HBV cccDNA reservoir, with durable biomarker repression observed up to 17 months.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Junshi Biosciences announced that the National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) has accepted New Drug Applications for toripalimab injection (subcutaneous) across 12 tumor indications. JS001sc is the first domestic anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody in a subcutaneous formulation to enter the marketing application stage in China.

AstraZeneca announced that the CARES Phase III clinical programme for anselamimab, a monoclonal antibody targeting amyloid fibrils, did not meet its primary endpoint in the overall light chain amyloidosis population. However, prespecified subgroup analysis showed a 62% improvement in survival and 71% reduction in cardiovascular hospitalizations for patients with kappa light chain amyloidosis.

Junshi Biosciences announced that the National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) has accepted new drug applications for its subcutaneous toripalimab injection (JS001sc) across 12 tumor indications. JS001sc is the first domestically developed anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody in a subcutaneous formulation to reach the marketing application stage in China.

bispecific antibodies.news: Pfizer and Innovent Biologics entered a global strategic collaboration to develop 12 oncology programs, including multi-specific antibodies with differentiated immune-engaging features. Pfizer secured exclusive global or outside-Greater China licenses for eight programs and will co-develop four programs with Innovent, involving a $650 million upfront payment.

AstraZeneca announced its strategy to investigate next-generation immunotherapies, explicitly including bispecific antibodies and T-cell engagers, to overcome tumor immune evasion. This initiative complements their existing portfolio anchored by Imfinzi and Imjudo.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Pfizer and Innovent Biologics entered a global strategic collaboration to develop 12 oncology programs, including multi-specific antibodies and antibody-drug conjugates. The deal includes an exclusive global license for four programs and co-development of four others, with Innovent receiving a $650 million upfront payment.

The FDA approved AstraZeneca's Imfinzi (durvalumab), a human monoclonal antibody, in combination with BCG for treating BCG-naïve, high-risk non-muscle-invasive bladder cancer. The approval is based on Phase III POTOMAC trial results showing a 32% reduction in the risk of disease recurrence, progression, or death.

gene editing.news: CRISPR Therapeutics announced its senior management team will participate in upcoming investor conferences in June 2026, including the Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference and Goldman Sachs Global Healthcare Conference. The company continues to advance its gene editing pipeline, including its proprietary SyNTase™ platform, following the historic approval of its CRISPR-based therapy CASGEVY.

bispecific antibodies.news: The U.S. FDA has approved DECNUPAZ (pivekimab sunirine-pvzy) for the treatment of adult patients with blastic plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm (BPDCN). This approval, supported by Phase 1/2 CADENZA trial data, marks AbbVie's first ADC approved for blood cancer, though the drug is technically an antibody-drug conjugate targeting CD123.

monoclonal antibodies.news: The U.S. FDA has approved DECNUPAZ (pivekimab sunirine-pvzy), a CD123-targeting antibody-drug conjugate (ADC), for the treatment of adult patients with blastic plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm (BPDCN). This approval, supported by data from the Phase 1/2 CADENZA trial, marks AbbVie's first ADC approved for blood cancer and the first of its kind for BPDCN that can be initiated in an outpatient setting.

GSK presented new data at ASCO and EHA 2026 highlighting long-term efficacy of belantamab mafodotin, a BCMA-directed antibody-drug conjugate, in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma. The company also showcased results for dostarlimab, a PD-1 blocking monoclonal antibody, predicting cure rates in endometrial cancer.

GSK presented new data at ASCO and EHA highlighting long-term efficacy of belantamab mafodotin (Blenrep), a BCMA-directed monoclonal antibody, in relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma. The company also showcased results for dostarlimab (Jemperli), a PD-1 blocking antibody, predicting cure rates in endometrial cancer.

Chugai Pharmaceutical received 'The Imperial Invention Prize' for its proprietary recycling antibody technology, which enhances the sustained efficacy of antibody drugs by enabling single molecules to bind multiple antigens.

Novartis presented late-breaking Phase III data for Fabhalta (iptacopan) and extended follow-up data for the investigational anti-APRIL therapy zigakibart at the ERA 2026 Congress. The presentations highlighted efficacy and safety outcomes in IgA nephropathy, reinforcing the company's portfolio strength in kidney disease treatments.

Chugai Pharmaceutical received 'The Imperial Invention Prize' for its proprietary recycling antibody technology, which enhances the sustained efficacy of antibody drugs by enabling a single molecule to bind multiple antigen molecules.

gene editing.news: Abiogenesis Clinpharm announced its entry into conducting clinical trials for Cell and Gene Therapy (CGT), marking a strategic expansion to support next-generation therapies including CRISPR-based gene editing programs.

monoclonal antibodies.news: GSK presented two-year efficacy and safety data for depemokimab (Exdensur) at ATS 2026, demonstrating sustained disease control in severe asthma patients with just two doses per year. The company also shared new analyses for mepolizumab (Nucala) highlighting its ability to achieve disease stability in a wide spectrum of COPD patients.

GSK presented new clinical data at ATS 2026 showing sustained efficacy over two years for depemokimab (Exdensur) in severe asthma patients with type 2 inflammation. Additionally, new analyses for mepolizumab (Nucala) demonstrated the ability to achieve disease stability across a wide spectrum of COPD patients.

Immunocore presented landmark five-year overall survival data for KIMMTRAK (tebentafusp) at the AACR 2026 meeting, showing it doubles the likelihood of patients with metastatic uveal melanoma being alive at five years compared to the control arm.
Immunocore reported first-quarter 2026 net sales of $106.7 million for KIMMTRAK, representing a 14% year-over-year increase driven by volume growth in the US and Europe and global market expansion.

UCB announced a new strategic alliance with Cancer Research UK and Cancer Research Horizons to accelerate the development of novel cancer therapies, nominating two additional antibody drug candidates from its pipeline for early clinical development.

bispecific antibodies.news: Immunocore presented landmark five-year overall survival data for KIMMTRAK, a bispecific TCR-antibody, at the AACR 2026 meeting, showing it doubled the likelihood of survival at five years for patients with metastatic uveal melanoma. The company also provided updates on the Phase 3 PRISM-MEL-301 trial for its PRAME-targeted bispecific candidate brenetafusp in combination with nivolumab for advanced cutaneous melanoma.

UCB announced a new strategic alliance with Cancer Research UK and Cancer Research Horizons to accelerate the development of novel cancer therapies, expanding on a previous collaboration that advanced two antibody drug candidates into clinical trials. Under the new agreement, UCB has nominated two additional pipeline projects for joint progression through discovery and preclinical stages, with plans for Phase 1/2 clinical trials sponsored by Cancer Research UK.

gene editing.news: CRISPR Therapeutics announced that its senior management team will present at the Bank of America Securities 2026 Global Healthcare Conference on May 12, 2026. The company continues to advance its pipeline of gene-based medicines, including its approved CRISPR-based therapy CASGEVY and its proprietary SyNTase editing platform.

UCB announced a new strategic alliance with Cancer Research UK and Cancer Research Horizons to accelerate the development of novel cancer therapies. The partnership expands on previous collaborations to advance investigational oncology candidates, including two new projects nominated from UCB's pipeline for progression through discovery and early clinical development.

bispecific antibodies.news: BigHat Biosciences demonstrated the world's first 'NOT'-gated, T cell-engaging multispecific antibody, utilizing AI/ML-enabled structural engineering to selectively target cancer cells while sparing healthy tissue based on specific genetic patterns.

monoclonal antibodies.news: AbbVie presented new long-term data at the 2026 Digestive Disease Week meeting demonstrating the sustained efficacy and safety of SKYRIZI (risankizumab), a monoclonal antibody targeting IL-23, for treating Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis.

AbbVie announced the submission of an application to the FDA on April 27, 2026, seeking approval for the subcutaneous induction formulation of SKYRIZI (risankizumab) for the treatment of adult patients with moderately to severely active Crohn's disease.

Disc Medicine announced updated data from the Phase 2 RALLY-MF study of DISC-0974, an anti-hemojuvelin monoclonal antibody, for anemia of myelofibrosis, which will be presented as an oral presentation at the ASCO Annual Meeting. The company also confirmed progress in the Phase 2 study of DISC-3405, an anti-TMPRSS6 monoclonal antibody, for polycythemia vera and sickle cell disease.

BigHat Biosciences demonstrated the world's first 'NOT'-gated, T cell-engaging multispecific antibody, utilizing AI/ML-enabled structural engineering to selectively target cancer cells while sparing healthy tissue.

gene editing.news: Tune Therapeutics announced late-breaking Phase 1b/2a clinical results for TUNE-401, an epigenetic silencer for chronic Hepatitis B, to be presented at the EASL Congress 2026. The data highlights the first clinical evidence of epigenetic silencing of the HBV cccDNA reservoir using the company's TEMPO platform.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Takeda announced positive topline results from the pivotal Phase 2/3 TAK-881-3001 clinical trial for TAK-881 in Primary Immunodeficiency Disease (PID). The study demonstrated pharmacokinetic comparability to the standard of care HYQVIA while offering reduced infusion volume and duration, with regulatory submissions expected in fiscal year 2026.

gene editing.news: CRISPR Therapeutics reported that Vertex completed the U.S. regulatory submission for CASGEVY approval in children ages 5 to 11 with sickle cell disease or beta thalassemia, receiving a Commissioner's National Priority Voucher. Additionally, the company received FDA clearance for its IND application for CTX310, an in vivo liver-directed gene editing therapy for severe hypertriglyceridemia.

The company announced the initiation of a third Phase 1 trial for its allogeneic CAR-T cell therapy zugo-cel (CTX112) in autoimmune neurologic diseases, including progressive multiple sclerosis and stiff person syndrome, following FDA clearance of the IND application.

bispecific antibodies.news: UCB announced a definitive agreement to acquire Candid Therapeutics for up to $2.2 billion to acquire cizutamig, a bispecific T-cell engager targeting BCMA and CD3 for autoimmune diseases.

monoclonal antibodies.news: UCB announced a definitive agreement to acquire Candid Therapeutics for up to $2.2 billion to expand its immunology pipeline with novel T-cell engagers. The deal includes Candid's lead asset, cizutamig, a bispecific antibody targeting BCMA and CD3 currently in multiple Phase 1 clinical studies for autoimmune diseases.

bispecific antibodies.news: In April 2026, China's National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) granted conditional approval to IMDELLTRA (tarlatamab), a DLL3-targeting bispecific T-cell engager (BiTE), for third-line extensive-stage small cell lung cancer.

Amgen reported positive topline results from a Phase 3 trial of BLINCYTO (blinatumomab), a CD19-targeting bispecific T-cell engager, in combination with low-intensity chemotherapy for older adults with newly diagnosed B-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia.

Amgen initiated multiple Phase 3 and Phase 1b clinical studies for xaluritamig (AMG 509), a first-in-class bispecific T-cell engager targeting STEAP1, for the treatment of metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer and Ewing sarcoma.
Merck initiated a pivotal Phase 2b/3 clinical trial evaluating MK-8748, an investigational bispecific Tie2 agonist/VEGF inhibitor, for the treatment of neovascular age-related macular degeneration.

monoclonal antibodies.news: The FDA proposed withdrawing approval of TAVNEOS (avacopan) due to lack of substantial evidence of effectiveness and hepatotoxicity concerns, though the drug remains on the market pending a potential hearing.

The European Commission approved UPLIZNA (inebilizumab) as an add-on therapy for generalized myasthenia gravis in adult patients who are anti-acetylcholine receptor or anti-muscle specific tyrosine kinase antibody positive.
Amgen is advancing daxdilimab, a first-in-class plasmacytoid dendritic cell depleting monoclonal antibody, to a registrational phase of development.
A Phase 2 study of AMG 732, an insulin-like growth factor-1 receptor targeting monoclonal antibody, is currently enrolling patients with moderate-to-severe active thyroid eye disease.
The FDA approved KEYTRUDA (pembrolizumab) and KEYTRUDA QLEX plus paclitaxel, with or without bevacizumab, for the treatment of certain adults with PD-L1+ platinum-resistant ovarian cancer.
The European Commission approved a KEYTRUDA regimen plus paclitaxel, with or without bevacizumab, for the treatment of adults with PD-L1+ platinum-resistant recurrent ovarian carcinoma.
The FDA granted priority review for a label update for KEYTRUDA QLEX based on Phase 2 trial results evaluating patient preference for subcutaneous administration over intravenous administration.

The FDA granted priority review for KEYTRUDA and KEYTRUDA QLEX, each with Padcev, for cisplatin-eligible patients with muscle-invasive bladder cancer based on positive Phase 3 KEYNOTE-B15 trial results.

The FDA accepted supplemental applications for WELIREG in combination with KEYTRUDA or KEYTRUDA QLEX for the adjuvant treatment of certain patients with renal cell carcinoma, setting a PDUFA date of June 19, 2026.
The FDA accepted supplemental applications for WELIREG plus Lenvima in certain previously treated patients with advanced renal cell carcinoma, setting a PDUFA date of October 4, 2026.

The European Medicines Agency's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human View adopted a positive opinion for an expanded indication for CAPVAXIVE for active immunization against invasive pneumococcal disease in certain children and adolescents.

The FDA Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee (ODAC) voted 7 to 1 to recommend approval of Truqap (capivasertib) in combination with abiraterone and androgen deprivation therapy for PTEN-deficient metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer, based on positive Phase III CAPItello-281 trial results.

bispecific antibodies.news: Genentech announced it will present extensive data at ARVO 2026 showcasing its ophthalmology portfolio, including real-world data for Vabysmo (faricimab), the first bispecific antibody approved for the eye targeting Ang-2 and VEGF-A.

Pfizer announced positive topline results from the Phase 3 MagnetisMM-5 trial for ELREXFIO (elranatamab), a BCMA-CD3 directed bispecific antibody, demonstrating a statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival for patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma.

Roche announced it will present extensive real-world and clinical data for Vabysmo (faricimab), an industry-leading bispecific antibody targeting Ang-2 and VEGF-A, at the ARVO 2026 annual meeting. The data includes findings from the VOYAGER study demonstrating retinal drying in nAMD and DME, as well as long-term effectiveness results.

Pfizer announced positive topline results from the Phase 3 MagnetisMM-5 trial for ELREXFIO (elranatamab), a BCMAxCD3 bispecific antibody, showing statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival for relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma patients compared to standard of care.

Pfizer announced positive topline results from the Phase 3 MagnetisMM-5 trial for ELREXFIO (elranatamab), a BCMA-CD3 bispecific antibody. The study met its primary endpoint, showing a statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival for patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma compared to standard-of-care therapy.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Genentech announced it will present extensive data at ARVO 2026 showcasing its ophthalmology portfolio, including new real-world data for Vabysmo and early results for the investigational monoclonal antibody vamikibart in treating uveitic macular edema.

Pfizer announced positive topline results from the Phase 3 MagnetisMM-5 trial for ELREXFIO (elranatamab), demonstrating a statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival for patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma compared to standard-of-care therapy.
Roche presented first results from the phase 2 ALLUVIUM and BARDENAS trials for vamikibart, an investigational intravitreal monoclonal antibody targeting IL-6 for uveitic macular edema, at ARVO 2026.

AbbVie submitted a New Drug Application to the FDA for Skyrizi (risankizumab), a monoclonal antibody, for subcutaneous induction in moderately to severely active Crohn's disease, supported by Phase 3 AFFIRM study data.
AbbVie submitted a New Drug Application to the FDA for Rinvoq (upadacitinib) for a new indication in severe alopecia areata, supported by Phase 3 UP-AA clinical program data.

bispecific antibodies.news: Galapagos and Gilead entered into a binding agreement to collaborate on advancing a first-in-class T-cell engager program for autoimmune diseases. The company also announced the name change to Lakefront Biotherapeutics effective May 8, 2026.

monoclonal antibodies.news: GSK announced new product approvals for Exdensur in the EU and China for severe asthma with an eosinophilic phenotype and nasal polyps, and for Nucala in the EU for COPD. Both Exdensur and Nucala are monoclonal antibodies targeting IL-5 pathways.

gene editing.news: Affinia Therapeutics announced it will present preclinical data on AFTX-201, an AAV gene therapy for BAG3-associated dilated cardiomyopathy, at the ASGCT 2026 Annual Meeting. The company also highlighted its proprietary capsid engineering and manufacturing data, alongside a poster on an epigenetic-editing modality for Myotonic Dystrophy Type 1 developed with Modalis Therapeutics.

Affinia Therapeutics will present preclinical data on AFTX-201 for BAG3-associated dilated cardiomyopathy and highlight a collaboration with Modalis Therapeutics on an epigenetic-editing modality for Myotonic Dystrophy Type 1 at the ASGCT 2026 Annual Meeting.

monoclonal antibodies.news: The FDA granted Breakthrough Therapy designation and priority review for Ianalumab (VAY736) for the treatment of Sjögren's disease following positive Phase III trial results demonstrating reduced disease activity.

The FDA approved Cosentyx (secukinumab) for the treatment of moderate to severe hidradenitis suppurativa in pediatric patients aged 12 years and older, making it the only IL-17A inhibitor approved for this population.
Novartis completed the acquisition of Excellergy to acquire Exl-111, a half-life extended, high-affinity anti-IgE monoclonal antibody in Phase I for allergic diseases.
Regulatory submissions for Cosentyx (secukinumab) for the treatment of polymyalgia rheumatica (PMR) were completed in the US, Europe, and Japan.

AbbVie submitted a regulatory application to the FDA for SKYRIZI (risankizumab-rzaa), a monoclonal antibody, seeking approval for subcutaneous induction in adults with moderately to severely active Crohn's disease. The submission is supported by positive data from the Phase 3 AFFIRM study, which evaluated the efficacy and safety of the subcutaneous formulation.

The US FDA approved the Saphnelo Pen, a new autoinjector for subcutaneous self-administration of anifrolumab, for treating adult patients with systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). This approval follows positive results from the Phase III TULIP-SC trial, offering patients a convenient once-weekly dosing option outside of clinical settings.

The US FDA approved the Saphnelo Pen, a new autoinjector for subcutaneous self-administration of anifrolumab, for treating adult patients with systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). This approval follows positive results from the Phase III TULIP-SC trial, offering patients a convenient once-weekly alternative to intravenous infusion.

gene editing.news: Astellas announced a strategic halt of AT132 (resamirigene bilparvovec) for X-linked myotubular myopathy and a redeployment of resources to ASP2957, a next-generation investigational gene therapy, resulting in a ¥16.4 billion impairment loss.

bispecific antibodies.news: WuXi Biologics announced the structural completion and arrival of key equipment at its Chengdu microbial commercial manufacturing site, designed to support the production of complex modalities including bispecific antibodies. The facility features a 15,000 L fermenter and will leverage the company's proprietary platforms like WuXiBody™ to manufacture these therapies at scale.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Chugai announced the approval of Lunsumio and Polivy combination therapy for an additional indication of relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma. Additionally, Enspryng presented Phase III results in myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein antibody-associated disease (MOGAD) at the 2026 American Academy of Neurology Annual Meeting.

molecular glues.news: Proxygen is advancing first-in-class induced proximity therapeutics, specifically molecular glue degraders targeting p300 and CDK12 to reprogram transcriptional dependencies in cancer.

gene editing.news: The European Medicines Agency's CHMP adopted a positive opinion recommending marketing authorization for Itvisma (intrathecal onasemnogene abeparvovec), a one-time gene replacement therapy for spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) in patients aged two years and older, based on data from the STEER study.

bispecific antibodies.news: WuXi Biologics received GMP certification from the South Korea Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (MFDS) for three facilities, enabling commercial manufacturing of a bispecific antibody therapeutic for biliary tract cancer.

WuXi Biologics announced that three of its manufacturing facilities in Wuxi received GMP certification from the South Korea Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (MFDS). This certification enables the company to provide end-to-end commercial manufacturing services for a bispecific antibody indicated for the treatment of biliary tract cancer.

monoclonal antibodies.news: WuXi Biologics received South Korea MFDS GMP certification for three facilities, enabling commercial manufacturing of bispecific antibody therapeutics, a modality built upon monoclonal antibody platforms.

The US FDA approved Dupixent (dupilumab), a fully human monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of children aged two to 11 years with uncontrolled chronic spontaneous urticaria. This approval marks the first biologic medicine for this specific pediatric indication and is based on data from the LIBERTY-CUPID clinical study program.

The US FDA approved Dupixent (dupilumab), a monoclonal antibody, for treating children aged 2 to 11 with uncontrolled chronic spontaneous urticaria. This approval, based on the LIBERTY-CUPID clinical study program, marks the first biologic indication for this age group for this condition.

Merck's KEYTRUDA (pembrolizumab), a PD-1 inhibitor monoclonal antibody, received FDA approval for treating platinum-resistant ovarian cancer in combination with paclitaxel, with or without bevacizumab, based on Phase 3 KEYNOTE-B96 trial data showing improved progression-free and overall survival.
Roche announced positive Phase III results for Enspryng (satralizumab) in MOGAD, reducing relapse risk by 68%, marking a potential first treatment for the condition.
Roche reported positive Phase III results for Gazyva/Gazyvaro (obinutuzumab) in primary membranous nephropathy, achieving complete remission at two years.
The FDA accepted a New Drug Application for Gazyva/Gazyvaro for the treatment of systemic lupus erythematosus based on Phase III ALLEGORY data.

AbbVie announced a $1.4 billion investment to build a new manufacturing campus in Durham, North Carolina, specifically to support the production of its immunology and oncology medicines, which include monoclonal antibodies.

The US FDA approved Dupixent (dupilumab), a monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of children aged two to 11 years with uncontrolled chronic spontaneous urticaria (CSU). This approval marks the first biologic medicine for this specific pediatric indication and is based on data from the LIBERTY-CUPID clinical study program.

The US FDA approved Sanofi's Tzield (teplizumab), a CD3-directed monoclonal antibody, for an expanded indication to delay the onset of stage 3 type 1 diabetes in children aged one year and older with stage 2 T1D.

The US FDA extended the target action date for its review of the biologics license application for Sarclisa (isatuximab) subcutaneous formulation to July 23, 2026, for the treatment of multiple myeloma.

Disc Medicine announced it will present data from the RALLY-MF Phase 2 trial of DISC-0974, an anti-hemojuvelin monoclonal antibody, at the ASCO Annual Meeting for treating anemia in myelofibrosis patients.

The US FDA approved Sanofi's Tzield (teplizumab-mzwv), a CD3-directed monoclonal antibody, for an expanded indication to delay the onset of stage 3 type 1 diabetes in children aged one year and older with stage 2 disease.

The FDA extended the target action date for its review of Sanofi's Sarclisa (isatuximab-irfc) subcutaneous formulation for multiple myeloma to July 23, 2026, while the EMA's CHMP adopted a positive opinion for the same subcutaneous version.

Genentech announced that its Phase III METEOROID study met its primary endpoint, showing Enspryng (satralizumab) reduced the risk of relapses by 68% in patients with myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein antibody-associated disease (MOGAD). The company plans to submit these data to regulatory authorities globally to seek approval for the first treatment for this rare autoimmune disease.

Pfizer presented updated Phase 2 data for PF-08634404 (PF'4404), a novel bispecific antibody targeting PD-1 and VEGF, as monotherapy in first-line PD-L1-expressing non-small cell lung cancer. The company also highlighted ongoing Phase 3 studies evaluating this bispecific antibody in combination with chemotherapy for NSCLC and colorectal cancer.

Roche announced that its humanized monoclonal antibody ENSPRYNG (satralizumab) met the primary endpoint in the Phase III METEOROID study for MOGAD, reducing relapse risk by 68% compared to placebo. Data will be submitted to regulatory authorities globally for potential approval as the first treatment for this rare autoimmune disease.
Merck & Co. and Eisai provided an update on the Phase 3 LITESPARK-012 trial evaluating first-line combination treatments for advanced renal cell carcinoma.

Chugai announced new data from the METEOROID study of Enspryng (satralizumab) for myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein antibody-associated disease (MOGAD), presented at the 2026 American Academy of Neurology Annual Meeting.
Disc Medicine announced it will present data from the RALLY-MF Phase 2 trial of DISC-0974, an anti-hemojuvelin antibody, for anemia in myelofibrosis at the ASCO Annual Meeting.

bispecific antibodies.news: Pfizer presented updated Phase 2 data for PF-08634404 (SSGJ-707), a novel bispecific antibody targeting PD-1 and VEGF, as monotherapy in first-line PD-L1-expressing non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) at ASCO 2026. The company also highlighted ongoing Phase 3 studies (Symbiotic-Lung-01 and Symbiotic-GI-03) evaluating the agent in combination with chemotherapy for NSCLC and colorectal cancer.

monoclonal antibodies.news: The FDA accepted Genentech's supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for Gazyva (obinutuzumab), a monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). The filing is based on positive Phase III ALLEGORY study data showing significant disease activity reduction compared to placebo, with a PDUFA decision expected by December 2026.

The FDA accepted Genentech's supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for Gazyva (obinutuzumab), a humanized monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). The filing is based on positive Phase III ALLEGORY study data showing significant disease activity reduction compared to placebo, with an approval decision expected by December 2026.

The U.S. FDA granted Priority Review to a supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for the combination of PADCEV (enfortumab vedotin) and Keytruda (pembrolizumab) for perioperative treatment of muscle-invasive bladder cancer regardless of cisplatin eligibility, based on Phase 3 EV-304 trial data.

The U.S. FDA accepted a supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for Gazyva/Gazyvaro (obinutuzumab) for the treatment of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE), based on positive Phase 3 ALLEGORY study results showing significant disease activity reduction.

A prespecified interim analysis of the Phase 3 I CAN trial demonstrated that Ultomiris (ravulizumab) met its primary endpoint, showing a statistically significant and clinically meaningful reduction in proteinuria in adults with immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN).

AstraZeneca announced that tozorakimab, a first-in-class IL-33-targeting monoclonal antibody, met its primary endpoint in the Phase III MIRANDA trial for COPD. The drug demonstrated a statistically significant reduction in moderate-to-severe exacerbations in former smokers and the overall population.

Kyowa Kirin announced the discontinuation of all ongoing clinical trials for rocatinlimab, an investigational anti-OX40 monoclonal antibody, due to emerging safety concerns regarding malignancies, including cases of Kaposi's sarcoma.

Kyowa Kirin terminated its collaboration with Amgen to regain full control of the global development and commercialization program for rocatinlimab, an anti-OX40 monoclonal antibody for atopic dermatitis, following a strategic portfolio prioritization by Amgen.

GSK announced that the National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) of China approved Blenrep (belantamab mafodotin), a monoclonal antibody-drug conjugate, in combination with bortezomib and dexamethasone for the treatment of adults with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma who have received at least one prior line of therapy.

AstraZeneca reported that tozorakimab, a first-in-class monoclonal antibody targeting IL-33, met its primary endpoint in the Phase III MIRANDA trial, demonstrating a statistically significant reduction in the annualized rate of moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations in former smokers and the overall population.

Immunocore presented five-year overall survival data for KIMMTRAK (tebentafusp-tebn), a bispecific TCR-CD3 protein, at the AACR 2026 meeting, showing it doubled the likelihood of being alive at five years compared to the control arm in first-line HLA-A*02:01-positive patients with metastatic uveal melanoma.

Kyowa Kirin announced the discontinuation of all ongoing clinical trials for rocatinlimab, an investigational anti-OX40 monoclonal antibody, due to emerging safety concerns regarding malignancies including Kaposi's sarcoma.
Kyowa Kirin terminated its collaboration with Amgen to regain full global control of the rocatinlimab development and commercialization program for atopic dermatitis.

GSK announced that the National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) of China has approved Blenrep (belantamab mafodotin), an antibody-drug conjugate targeting BCMA, for the treatment of relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma in combination with bortezomib and dexamethasone.

AstraZeneca reported positive results from the Phase III MIRANDA trial for tozorakimab, an IL-33-targeting monoclonal antibody, which met its primary endpoint by significantly reducing the annualized rate of moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations.

bispecific antibodies.news: Immunocore presented five-year overall survival data for KIMMTRAK (tebentafusp), a TCR-bispecific protein, at the AACR 2026 meeting. The data showed that KIMMTRAK doubled the likelihood of being alive at five years compared to the control arm in patients with metastatic uveal melanoma.

Immunocore presented five-year overall survival data for KIMMTRAK (tebentafusp), a TCR-bispecific protein, at the AACR 2026 meeting, showing it doubled the likelihood of survival at five years compared to the control arm in patients with metastatic uveal melanoma.

Gilead Sciences received all required regulatory approvals to proceed with its acquisition of Arcellx, a company specializing in cell therapies and bispecific antibodies. The company extended its tender offer for Arcellx shares to $115.00 per share plus contingent value rights, pending the expiration of a 14-day waiting period from Australian competition authorities.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Gilead Sciences received all required regulatory approvals to proceed with its acquisition of Arcellx, a company developing bispecific antibodies and CAR-T therapies, and extended the tender offer deadline to April 27, 2026.

gene editing.news: Gilead Sciences received all required regulatory approvals to proceed with its acquisition of Arcellx, a company developing anito-cel, a CAR-T cell therapy for multiple myeloma. The tender offer was extended to April 27, 2026, with a purchase price of $115.00 per share plus contingent value rights tied to future sales milestones.

UCB announced a definitive agreement to acquire Neurona Therapeutics for up to $1.15 billion to advance regenerative cell therapies for epilepsy. The deal includes NRTX-1001, a neuronal cell therapy candidate currently in Phase I/II trials, which utilizes pluripotent stem cell technology to restore neural circuitry.

bispecific antibodies.news: Hinge Bio announced the first subject dosed in a Phase 1 clinical trial of HB2198, a novel multispecific antibody candidate targeting CD19 and CD20 for treating autoimmune diseases. The company has initiated two Phase 1 trials: one in healthy volunteers to assess safety and tolerability, and another in patients with Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE).

monoclonal antibodies.news: GSK completed the acquisition of 35Pharma Inc. for $950 million, gaining access to HS235, a novel protein-based therapeutic targeting the activin receptor signaling pathway for pulmonary hypertension. While the press release describes HS235 as a protein-based therapeutic, the acquisition expands GSK's respiratory and immunology pipeline with a differentiated mechanism of action.

Hinge Bio announced the first subject dosed in a Phase 1 clinical trial of HB2198, a novel multispecific antibody candidate targeting CD19 and CD20 for treating autoimmune diseases. The company has initiated two Phase 1 trials, one in healthy volunteers and another in patients with Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE).

molecular glues.news: STORM Therapeutics secured $56 million in Series C financing and dosed the first patient in a Phase 2 sarcoma trial for STC-15, a first-in-class METTL3 inhibitor that functions as a molecular glue to reprogram malignant progenitor cells.

monoclonal antibodies.news: BigHat Biosciences highlights its in-house cell-free protein synthesis (CFPS) manufacturing capabilities, which enable the overnight expression and rapid characterization of thousands of antibodies using its Milliner platform.

The European Commission approved Dupixent (dupilumab), a monoclonal antibody, for treating moderate-to-severe chronic spontaneous urticaria in children aged two to 11 years, based on data from the LIBERTY-CUPID clinical study program.

The European Commission approved Dupixent (dupilumab), a monoclonal antibody, as the first targeted medicine to treat children aged 2 to 11 with chronic spontaneous urticaria. This approval expands the drug's indication in the EU based on data from the LIBERTY-CUPID clinical study program.

GSK presented positive phase I data for mocertatug rezetecan, an antibody-drug conjugate featuring a fully human anti-B7-H4 monoclonal antibody, showing high response rates in ovarian and endometrial cancers. These results support the initiation of five pivotal phase III trials in 2026.

AbbVie showcased late-breaking phase 2 data for mirvetuximab soravtansine-gynx (ELAHERE), an antibody-drug conjugate containing a folate receptor alpha-binding antibody, demonstrating a 62.7% objective response rate in platinum-sensitive ovarian cancer. The data supports further investigation of the drug in combination with carboplatin.

bispecific antibodies.news: Alexion presented new data from the PREVAIL Phase III trial evaluating gefurulimab, a novel dual-binding nanobody, for the treatment of adults with anti-acetylcholine receptor antibody-positive generalized myasthenia gravis at the 2026 AAN Annual Meeting.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Alexion presented new data at the 2026 AAN Annual Meeting from the PREVAIL Phase III trial, showing statistically significant improvements with the investigational C5 inhibitor gefurulimab for adults with generalized myasthenia gravis. The company also shared results on ravulizumab (Ultomiris) in neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder and selumetinib (Koselugo) for neurofibromatosis type 1.

gene editing.news: Cartherics and Catalent expanded their commercial license agreement to utilize a Catalent cGMP-compliant induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC) line for manufacturing CAR-NK cell therapies. The partnership leverages Catalent's validated workflows for gene editing to support the development of Cartherics' lead product, CTH-401, for ovarian cancer and endometriosis.

Généthon scientists published a study in Molecular Therapy detailing a CRISPR/Cas9 strategy to increase utrophin expression in Duchenne muscular dystrophy by disabling a microRNA binding site. The approach demonstrated improved muscle function and reduced fibrosis in preclinical models, offering a potential universal therapy independent of specific DMD mutations.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Chugai Pharmaceutical and Zenyaku Kogyo announced the termination of their co-promotion agreement for Rituxan (rituximab), an anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody, in Japan effective September 3, 2026. Following the transition, Zenyaku Kogyo will assume sole responsibility for sales and medical information activities for the product.

GSK announced that China's NMPA approved Exdensur (depemokimab), an ultra-long-acting IL-5 monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps (CRSwNP). The approval is based on data from the ANCHOR-1 and ANCHOR-2 phase III trials.

GSK announced that China's National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) has approved Exdensur (depemokimab), an ultra-long-acting anti-IL-5 monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps (CRSwNP). The approval is based on data from the ANCHOR-1 and ANCHOR-2 phase III trials showing significant improvements in nasal polyp size and obstruction.

Gilead Sciences entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Tubulis GmbH for $3.15 billion in upfront cash and up to $1.85 billion in milestone payments. The acquisition adds Tubulis' next-generation antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) platform and clinical-stage assets, including TUB-040 and TUB-030, to Gilead's oncology pipeline.

Evotec secured a multi-year BioMaP-Consortium award from BARDA valued at up to $10 million to optimize the biomanufacturing of monoclonal antibodies against Ebola and Sudan viruses using its continuous manufacturing platform.

Evotec received a new Gates Foundation grant to advance global access to affordable biotherapeutics by applying its AI-driven J.MD molecular design platform to improve monoclonal antibody developability for high-priority global health indications.

molecular glues.news: Evotec's CELMoD molecular glue candidate BMS-986506 progressed from IND acceptance in November 2025 into a Phase 1 clinical study in March 2026 for renal cell carcinoma, in collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb.

gene editing.news: CRISPR Therapeutics announced that senior management will present at the 25th Annual Needham Virtual Healthcare Conference on April 13, 2026. The company highlighted its leadership in gene editing, including the approved CASGEVY therapy and its proprietary SyNTase editing platform.

bispecific antibodies.news: Sanofi announced that its bispecific Nanobody lunsekimig met primary and key secondary endpoints in Phase 2 studies for moderate-to-severe asthma (AIRCULES) and chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps (DUET). However, the drug failed to meet its primary endpoint in an exploratory Phase 2 study for atopic dermatitis (VELVET).

Immunocore announced that its management team will present at the 25th Annual Needham Virtual Healthcare Conference to discuss its pipeline of TCR bispecific immunotherapies (ImmTAX), including its approved product KIMMTRAK for uveal melanoma.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Sanofi announced that lunsekimig, a novel bispecific Nanobody (VHH) targeting TSLP and IL-13, met primary and key secondary endpoints in Phase 2 studies for asthma and chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps (CRSwNP), though it failed to meet the primary endpoint in atopic dermatitis.

Amgen reported positive topline results from a Phase 3 trial for subcutaneous TEPEZZA (teprotumumab), a monoclonal antibody for Thyroid Eye Disease, which met its primary endpoint with a 77% proptosis response rate and demonstrated efficacy comparable to the intravenous formulation.

gene editing.news: Affinia Therapeutics received approval from Health Canada to initiate the UPBEAT trial, a Phase 1/2 clinical study investigating AFTX-201, an AAV gene therapy for BAG3-associated dilated cardiomyopathy. This follows FDA acceptance of the IND and Fast Track designation for the investigational medicine.

Affinia Therapeutics received approval from Health Canada to initiate the UPBEAT trial, a Phase 1/2 clinical study investigating AFTX-201, an AAV gene therapy for BAG3-associated dilated cardiomyopathy. This follows FDA acceptance of the IND application and Fast Track designation for the investigational medicine.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Lipocine reported topline safety and efficacy results for LPCN 1154 (oral brexanolone) in a Phase 3 trial for postpartum depression. While the primary endpoint was not met in the overall population, a post hoc analysis in patients with a history of psychiatric conditions showed statistically significant and clinically meaningful reductions in HAM-D scores. The drug demonstrated a favorable safety profile with no severe adverse events.

gene editing.news: Caris Life Sciences launched Caris ChromoSeq, the world's first Whole Genome and Whole Transcriptome Tumor Profiling assay specifically designed for myeloid malignancies. The test aims to replace fragmented diagnostic workflows with a single, integrated solution for acute myeloid leukemia, myelodysplastic syndromes, and myeloproliferative neoplasms.

bispecific antibodies.news: Positive results from the EMERALD-3 Phase III trial showed that Imfinzi (durvalumab) plus Imjudo (tremelimumab), combined with lenvatinib and TACE, significantly improved progression-free survival in patients with unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma eligible for embolization.
